'''James McDonnell''' (born 12 January 2000) is an {{rlnt|Ireland}} international [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
McDonnell played his amateur rugby league for [[Wigan St Judes]].{{cn|date=July 2025}}

==Club career==
===Wigan Warriors===
McDonnell made his Super League debut in round 14 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]] for the Warriors against [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]] where Wigan went on to lose 42–0 against a much more experienced St Helens squad. McDonnell started the match playing at {{rlp|ce}} and became Wigan Warriors player #1106.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1587/round-14-fixtures-confirmed|title=Round 14 fixtures confirmed |publisher=Sky Sports |date=22 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/wigan-vs-st-helens/teams/54569 |title=Starting Lineups - Wigan vs St Helens |publisher=Sky Sports |date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/54330327 |title=Super League: Wigan vs St Helens |publisher=BBC |date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/debut-a-moment-ill-never-forget-says-kai-pearce-paul/ |title=Debut a moment "I'll never forget" says Kai Pearce Paul |publisher=Love Rugby League |date=29 September 2020}}</ref>

====York City Knights (loan)====
On 14 May 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on one-month loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=York Press|date=14 May 2021|accessdate=14 May 2021|title=York City Knights loan in Wigan Warriors forward James McDonnell|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/19302466.york-city-knights-loan-wigan-warriors-forward-james-mcdonnell/}}</ref>

====Leigh Centurions (DR)====
McDonnell spent the 2022 season on dual registration with [[Leigh Centurions]].{{cn|date=October 2022}}

===Leeds===
McDonnell played 23 matches for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-2023-season-review-picking-out-the-best-and-worst-players-performances-results-4355920/|title=Leeds Rhinos' 2023 season review: picking out the best and worst players, performances, results|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>
McDonnell played 25 games for Leeds in the 2024 Super League which saw the club finish 8th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/from-310-to-810-peter-smiths-leeds-rhinos-player-ratings-for-2024-season-4799226/|title=Leeds Rhinos player ratings from the 2024 Super League season|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>

===Perth Bears===
On 12 January 2026 it was reported that McDonnell had signed with the [[Perth Bears]] on a two-year deal at the end of the 2026 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=12 Jan 2026|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Leeds Rhinos’ James McDonnell signs two-year deal with Perth Bears|url=https://www.totalrl.com/james-mcdonnell-signs-two-year-deal-with-perth-bears/}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2026/01/01/2026-nrl-signings-tracker-the-latest-from-all-17-clubs/|title=2026 NRL Signings Tracker: Crichton to depart Roosters; Colquhoun re-signs|website=www.nrl.com}}</ref>

==International career==
McDonnell received his first international call up for the [[England Knights]] in early 2022. Later that year he was called up to represent [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cn|date=October 2022}}
